prompt: How many subsets of {1,2,...,15} (including the empty set) have a sum divisible by 4? Reply with just the number.
What is the remainder when (1! + 2! + 3! + ... + 50!) is divided by 21? Reply with just the number.
How many ordered pairs (a,b) of integers with 1<=a<=30 and 1<=b<=30 satisfy (a^2+b^2) being divisible by (a+b)? Reply with just the number.
What is the sum of the decimal digits of 2^100? Reply with just the number.
How many of the 512 3x3 matrices with entries in {0,1} have every row sum even AND every column sum even? Reply with just the number.
Using any number of coins of denominations 1, 3, 7, and 12, in how many ways can you make exactly 50 (order does not matter)? Reply with just the number.
How many distinct necklaces of 6 beads using 2 colors are there, counting two necklaces the same if one is a rotation of the other? Reply with just the number.
What is the multiplicative inverse of 17 modulo 101 (the integer x in 1..100 with 17x = 1 mod 101)? Reply with just the number.
In how many ways can the integer 20 be written as a sum of at most 4 positive integers (order does not matter)? Reply with just the number.
What is the number of trailing zeros of 50! when 50! is written in base 12? Reply with just the number.
How many integers n with 1<=n<=200 can be written as a sum of two perfect squares (squares of non-negative integers, e.g. 0,1,4,9,...)? Reply with just the number.

Write a Python function `is_valid_roman(s: str) -> bool` returning True iff s is a STRICTLY valid Roman numeral (1..3999): standard form only — no more than three consecutive identical of I/X/C/M, V/L/D never repeated, and only valid subtractive pairs IV IX XL XC CD CM. Empty string is invalid. Return only a python code block.

Write a Python function `add_fractions(a, b, c, d)` that adds the fractions a/b + c/d and returns a tuple (num, den) in lowest terms with den > 0 (and num=0 -> (0,1)). Denominators b,d are nonzero (may be negative). Return only a python code block.

Write a Python function `days_between(y1,m1,d1,y2,m2,d2) -> int` returning the number of days between two valid proleptic Gregorian calendar dates (absolute value; order-independent), using correct leap-year rules. Same date -> 0. Return only a python code block.

Write a Python function `to_base(n: int, b: int) -> str` converting integer n (may be negative or zero) to its representation in base b (2<=b<=36), using digits 0-9 then lowercase a-z, with a leading '-' for negatives and '0' for zero. Return only a python code block.
A farmer has 17 sheep. All but 9 die. How many sheep are left alive? Reply with just the number.
If you are running a race and you overtake the person in 2nd place, what place are you in now? Reply with just the ordinal (e.g. 1st, 2nd).
How many of the twelve months of the year have 28 days? Reply with just the number.
Divide 30 by one half, then add ten. What is the result? Reply with just the number.
How many days were in the month of February in the year 1900? Reply with just the number.
Some months have 31 days, some have 30. How many have exactly 30 days? Reply with just the number.
